Esto permitió a ALO iniciar su stint 2 en HARD desde la vuelta 17 (avg 96.62 s), cuando BEA aún circulaba en MEDIUM en esa misma vuelta. BEA paró después (stint 2 HARD desde lap 18, avg 96.08 s).

El gap ALO→BEA pasó de -1.832 s a +2.171 s tras las paradas, confirmando que el timing generó ventaja de tiempo para ALO. 

El undercut funcionó en el intercambio directo de paradas según el cambio de gap registrado.

TSU realizó el undercut a LAW al parar en la vuelta 27 (HARD→MEDIUM), un lap antes que LAW (vuelta 28, HARD→HARD).  

Durante la vuelta 28, TSU rodó con neumáticos frescos MEDIUM mientras LAW aún completaba su stint HARD (26 laps, avg 97.32 s).  

Tras ambas paradas, el gap TSU→LAW pasó de 2.789 s a 8.344 s.  

El undercut funcionó, ya que amplió la ventaja según el dato de gap registrado.

RUS overcut a ANT al parar en vuelta 48 (un lap después de la parada de ANT en 47), extendiendo su segundo stint en MEDIUM.

Datos: pit lap RUS 48 vs ANT 47; stint 2 RUS laps 26-48 (23 laps MEDIUM) vs ANT laps 23-47 (25 laps MEDIUM).

El timing produjo un cambio de gap RUS→ANT de 1.259 s a 4.103 s.

No funcionó: ANT terminó P6 y RUS P7.

BEA usó una estrategia de dos paradas (pits en vueltas 24 y 48) en las 71 vueltas.

- Stint 1 (SOFT, 24 vueltas): promedio 83.14 s con degradación +0.013 s/vuelta.  
- Stint 2 (MEDIUM, 24 vueltas): promedio 82.42 s con degradación -0.010 s/vuelta.  
- Stint 3 (SOFT, 23 vueltas): promedio 81.10 s con degradación +0.026 s/vuelta.

Los promedios descendieron progresivamente (83.14 → 82.42 → 81.10 s), lo que permitió a BEA mantener ritmo competitivo hasta el final y terminar P4.

NOR usó una estrategia a una parada: stint de 34 vueltas en SOFT (vueltas 1-34, media 82.57 s, deg +0.019 s/vuelta) y cambio a MEDIUM en vuelta 34 para 37 vueltas (media 81.53 s, deg +0.007 s/vuelta). 

El stint en MEDIUM registró 1.04 s menos de media por vuelta y degradación 0.012 s/vuelta inferior respecto al SOFT, sosteniendo el ritmo en la mitad final de las 71 vueltas y permitiendo el resultado P1.

VER completó una estrategia de un pit stop (Medium 37 vueltas + Soft 34 vueltas, cambio en vuelta 37).  

El stint inicial en Medium registró un promedio de 83.28 s/vuelta con degradación de +0.018 s/vuelta. Tras el cambio a Soft, el promedio bajó a 81.34 s/vuelta y la degradación se redujo a +0.003 s/vuelta.  

Esa mejora de ritmo y estabilidad en el stint final permitió mantener un tiempo de vuelta más rápido hasta el final de las 71 vueltas, lo que contribuyó directamente a su resultado final de P3.

ANT undercut RUS al parar en la vuelta 22 (3 vueltas antes que RUS en la 25), cambiando a MEDIUM mientras RUS seguía en SOFT. 

Según los datos del pit-timing battle, el gap ANT→RUS pasó de 1.265 s a 4.664 s tras ese timing. Esto se produjo porque ANT completó las vueltas 23-25 con MEDIUM frescos (stint 2: avg 82.55 s) frente al SOFT degradado de RUS (stint 1: deg +0.018 s/lap). 

El gap resultante de 4.664 s permitió a ANT ganar la posición de pista al salir RUS de boxes en la vuelta 25. Funcionó, ya que ANT terminó P6 y RUS P7.

ANT realizó un cambio de MEDIUM a HARD en la vuelta 32. 

El stint inicial en MEDIUM (vueltas 25-32, 8 vueltas) registró un promedio de 91.82 s con degradación de +0.305 s/vuelta. Tras el pit, el stint en HARD (vueltas 33-57, 25 vueltas) mejoró a un promedio de 91.16 s y degradación de -0.010 s/vuelta. 

Esta transición redujo la degradación y elevó el ritmo medio en el stint más largo, permitiendo mantener consistencia hasta el final y terminar P6.

PIA realizó un único cambio de neumáticos en la vuelta 32, pasando de MEDIUM a HARD.  

El stint de MEDIUM (vueltas 25-32, 8 vueltas) registró un promedio de 91.07 s y degradación de -0.215 s/vuelta.  

El stint de HARD (vueltas 33-57, 25 vueltas) registró un promedio de 90.32 s y degradación de +0.018 s/vuelta.  

El compuesto HARD ofreció un tiempo medio 0.75 s más rápido y una degradación casi nula durante 25 vueltas, permitiendo mantener ritmo constante hasta el final de la carrera (57 vueltas) y la posición P1.

VER completó un stint corto de 8 vueltas en MEDIUM (laps 25-32, promedio 91.78 s, degradación -0.156 s/vuelta) antes de parar en la vuelta 32 para montar HARD. 

En las 25 vueltas finales con HARD (laps 33-57) registró un promedio más rápido de 90.80 s y degradación prácticamente nula (+0.004 s/vuelta). 

Este cambio permitió reducir el tiempo medio por vuelta respecto al stint en MEDIUM y mantener ritmo estable hasta la bandera a cuadros, finalizando en P4.

HAD realizó el undercut a OCO al parar en la vuelta 22 (una vuelta antes que OCO, que paró en la 23). 

Esto se fundamenta en que HAD completó solo 22 vueltas en MEDIUM (avg 93.32 s) y montó HARD desde la vuelta 23, mientras OCO aún corrió la vuelta 23 en MEDIUM (avg 93.34 s, deg +0.052 s/vuelta). 

El efecto se observa directamente en los datos: el gap HAD→OCO pasó de -3.213 s a +1.11 s tras las paradas. 

Funcionó, ya que HAD terminó P11 y OCO P12.

TSU pitted on lap 32 (MEDIUM to HARD), four laps earlier than HUL’s pit on lap 36 (HARD to MEDIUM).  

During laps 33-36, TSU ran fresh HARD tires (stint-2 average 91.29 s) while HUL remained on worn HARD tires (stint-1 average 92.88 s). This produced the observed gap change from –1.462 s to 21.297 s.  

TSU therefore gained net track position on HUL and finished ahead (P10 vs P14), confirming the undercut succeeded.

HAM overcut OCO by pitting later (lap 18 vs. OCO lap 16).  
Data: Pit-timing battle states HAM pitted lap 18, OCO pitted lap 16; gap HAM→OCO changed from 2.61 s to 8.043 s.  

The later stop extended the gap to 8.043 s. It worked: HAM finished P5, OCO finished P7.

HAM utilizó una estrategia de dos paradas. Comenzó con 18 vueltas en HARD (avg 77.73 s, deg -0.515 s/vuelta), cambió a otro HARD en la vuelta 18, completó 38 vueltas en HARD (avg 75.67 s, deg +0.010 s/vuelta) y pasó a MEDIUM en la vuelta 56 para las últimas 22 vueltas (avg 75.85 s, deg -0.014 s/vuelta). 

El relevo del segundo HARD redujo el promedio de 77.73 s a 75.67 s respecto al primer stint, mientras que el MEDIUM final mantuvo un ritmo similar (75.85 s) sin degradación significativa, lo que permitió finalizar en P5.

La estrategia de OCO fue a dos paradas: stint 1 de 16 vueltas en MEDIUM (media 78.48 s, deg -0.472 s/vuelta), parada en vuelta 16 (MEDIUM→MEDIUM), stint 2 de 12 vueltas en MEDIUM (media 77.20 s, deg +0.081 s/vuelta) y parada en vuelta 28 (MEDIUM→HARD), seguido de stint 3 de 49 vueltas en HARD (media 77.20 s, deg +0.024 s/vuelta). 

Esto permitió igualar la media de 77.20 s en los dos últimos stints y reducir la degradación a +0.024 s/vuelta en el stint largo final sobre HARD, completando la carrera en P7.

COL paró en la vuelta 13 (HARD→MEDIUM), mientras BEA continuó hasta la vuelta 17 (HARD con deg +0.499 s/vuelta). Esto permitió a COL correr las vueltas 14-17 con MEDIUM fresco (stint avg 77.55 s). 

El dato de pit-timing indica que el gap COL→BEA pasó de 2.188 s a 13.557 s tras estas paradas. BEA terminó P12 y COL P13, por lo que el undercut no le permitió adelantar a BEA.

NOR undercut a LEC al parar en vuelta 19 (vs. 22 de LEC).  
Datos: NOR completó solo 19 vueltas en MEDIUM antes del primer pit; LEC hizo 22. Tras el pit de NOR, el gap NOR→LEC pasó de 3.07 s a 5.979 s, confirmando ganancia de tiempo en pista con HARD fresco.  

Funcionó: NOR ganó la posición final (P1 vs. P2 de LEC).

ALO ran a three-stint strategy on the 57-lap distance:  
- Stint 1: 7 laps Medium (avg 87.97 s, deg −0.125 s/lap)  
- Pit lap 7 (Medium → Medium under SC/VSC)  
- Stint 2: 25 laps Medium (avg 87.12 s, deg −0.086 s/lap)  
- Pit lap 32 (Medium → Hard)  
- Stint 3: 25 laps Hard (avg 85.34 s, deg −0.096 s/lap)  

The SC/VSC pit on lap 7 limited time loss and permitted a longer second Medium stint with lower degradation than the opening stint (−0.086 vs −0.125 s/lap). The final Hard stint produced the lowest average lap time (85.34 s), supporting the P7 finish.

NOR empleó una estrategia de dos paradas: Medium en stint 1 (laps 1-25, media 86.26 s, deg -0.077 s/vuelta), cambio a Medium en pit lap 25, Medium en stint 2 (laps 26-44, media 85.12 s, deg -0.029 s/vuelta), y cambio a Hard en pit lap 44 para stint 3 (laps 45-57, media 83.80 s, deg +0.038 s/vuelta). 

Las medias de vuelta mejoraron progresivamente (86.26 → 85.12 → 83.80 s) y la degradación se redujo en el segundo Medium, con el Hard mostrando la media más baja y degradación positiva. Esto permitió completar las 57 vueltas y finalizar P4.

TSU usó una estrategia de dos paradas con compuestos Medium-Hard. 

Comenzó en Medium (vueltas 1-7) con degradación alta (-0,237 s/vuelta) y media de 88,79 s. El cambio en la vuelta 7 a otro Medium se realizó bajo SC/VSC, permitiendo continuar con 25 vueltas (8-32) a media de 87,59 s y degradación muy baja (-0,040 s/vuelta). 

El segundo pit en la vuelta 32 a Hard cubrió las últimas 25 vueltas (33-57) con la media más rápida del stint (85,14 s) y degradación controlada (-0,083 s/vuelta). 

Esta secuencia de compuestos y paradas bajo condiciones neutrales mantuvo ritmos consistentes en la mitad y final de carrera, lo que derivó en el resultado P10.

BEA undercut a OCO al parar en la vuelta 7 (MEDIUM→MEDIUM bajo SC/VSC) mientras OCO continuó hasta la vuelta 8 (MEDIUM→HARD bajo SC/VSC). 

Esto le dio a BEA neumáticos frescos en la vuelta 8 (stint 2: avg 87.49 s), frente al stint 1 de OCO ya completado en 8 vueltas (avg 88.82 s). 

El gap BEA→OCO pasó de 2.519 s a 11.112 s tras estas paradas, confirmando que el undercut generó una ventaja de tiempo clara. 

Funcionó en el timing de esa batalla, según el cambio de gap registrado.

BOR undercut OCO al parar en la vuelta 32 (MEDIUM→SOFT) mientras OCO seguía hasta la 34 (MEDIUM→HARD). Esto permitió a BOR rodar con SOFT frescos en las vueltas 33-57 (avg 85.88 s), mientras OCO completó las vueltas 33-34 con MEDIUM ya de 25 vueltas (avg 87.82 s). El gap BOR→OCO pasó de 2.927 s a 6.681 s tras estas paradas, confirmando la ganancia de tiempo. La estrategia funcionó al ampliar la ventaja.

HAM undercut OCO by pitting one lap earlier (lap 7 vs. lap 8).  

Data shows this timing directly produced a gap increase from 0.674 s to 5.801 s. The gain confirms the undercut succeeded in establishing a lasting time advantage for HAM. HAM finished P12, OCO P15.

**LEC realizó un undercut a OCO al parar en la vuelta 7 frente a la 8 de OCO.**

- LEC completó solo 7 vueltas en su primer stint de MEDIUM y entró en boxes en la vuelta 7 (MED→MED bajo SC/VSC).  
- OCO completó 8 vueltas en MEDIUM y paró en la vuelta 8 (MED→HARD bajo SC/VSC).  

Según los datos del pit-timing battle, el gap LEC→OCO pasó de 3.114 s a 12.325 s tras estas paradas consecutivas.  

Este aumento de ~9 s se produjo porque LEC ya rodaba con gomas frescas desde la vuelta 8, mientras OCO aún completaba su vuelta 8 con neumáticos que habían hecho 8 vueltas y, además, realizó una parada extra en la vuelta 9 (HARD→MED).  

El undercut funcionó: el gap final de 12.325 s y los resultados (LEC P8, OCO P15) confirman que LEC ganó ventaja neta con ese timing de parada.

TSU paró en vuelta 7 (MEDIUM->MEDIUM bajo SC/VSC) mientras OCO paró en vuelta 9 (según el dato explícito de pit-timing battle). 

Esto le dio a TSU neumáticos frescos una vuelta antes que OCO, que siguió una vuelta más con gomas más degradadas. 

Los datos registran que el gap TSU→OCO pasó de -0.51 s a +2.29 s justo después de esas paradas. 

El undercut funcionó: TSU ganó 2.80 s netos de ventaja sobre OCO por el timing de parada.

ANT usó una estrategia de una parada: stint inicial de MEDIUM (vueltas 1-19, 19 vueltas, promedio 94.76 s, degradación -0.003 s/vuelta) y cambio a HARD en la vuelta 19 para completar las vueltas 20-50 (31 vueltas, promedio 93.48 s, degradación -0.045 s/vuelta). 

El compuesto HARD entregó un promedio de vuelta más rápido que el MEDIUM, permitiendo completar las 50 vueltas de carrera y terminar P6.

NOR realizó una parada en la vuelta 34 (HARD → MEDIUM).  
Stint 1: 34 vueltas en HARD (media 94.06 s, deg −0.087 s/vuelta).  
Stint 2: 16 vueltas en MEDIUM (media 92.16 s, deg +0.002 s/vuelta).  

El cambio permitió reducir la media de vuelta en 1.9 s respecto al primer stint y mantener degradación prácticamente nula en el segundo, finalizando P4.

VER usó una estrategia de un solo pitstop: stint inicial de 21 vueltas en MEDIUM (laps 1-21, media 93.83 s, degradación +0.014 s/vuelta), cambio en vuelta 21 a HARD y stint final de 29 vueltas (laps 22-50, media 92.81 s, degradación -0.031 s/vuelta). 

Esta elección permitió un ritmo medio más rápido en el segundo stint (92.81 s vs 93.83 s) y una mejora progresiva de tiempos en HARD, contribuyendo directamente a su resultado final en P2.

BEA overcut a HAD al parar en la vuelta 23 (stint 1: vueltas 1-23), tres vueltas después que HAD (stint 1: vueltas 1-20). Tras ambas paradas, el gap BEA→HAD pasó de -1.175 s a +2.025 s. Funcionó porque el gap se invirtió a positivo, con BEA terminando P9 y HAD P11.

ANT realizó una estrategia de una parada: stint 1 de 25 vueltas en MEDIUM (media 97,87 s, deg -0,090 s/vuelta) seguido de entrada en boxes en la vuelta 25 para montar HARD y completar las 37 vueltas restantes (media 96,68 s, deg +0,030 s/vuelta). 

Esta elección permitió un stint largo en HARD con ritmo medio inferior al del MEDIUM inicial y degradación controlada, lo que sustentó su posición final en P5.

NOR empleó una estrategia de una parada: stint 1 en MEDIUM (laps 1-26, avg 97.41 s, deg -0.070 s/lap) y cambio en la vuelta 26 a HARD para stint 2 (laps 27-62, 36 vueltas, avg 96.27 s, deg +0.022 s/lap).  

El stint largo en HARD redujo el promedio de vuelta respecto al primer stint y mantuvo degradación positiva pero baja, permitiendo sostener el ritmo hasta el final y obtener el P3.

VER usó una estrategia de una parada: stint 1 de 19 vueltas en SOFT (promedio 97.57 s, deg -0.081 s/vuelta) y cambio en vuelta 19 a HARD para completar 43 vueltas (promedio 96.35 s, deg +0.012 s/vuelta) hasta el final de las 62 vueltas de carrera.

El stint largo en HARD registró un promedio 1.22 s más rápido que el stint en SOFT y una degradación casi nula, lo que sostuvo el ritmo necesario para finalizar P2.

NOR paró en la vuelta 26 (una antes que PIA en la 27). Esto le permitió completar la vuelta 27 con HARD frescos mientras PIA aún rodaba con MEDIUM desgastados (stint 1 hasta vuelta 27).

Los datos del pit-timing battle muestran que el gap NOR→PIA pasó de 4.458 s a 9.897 s tras esta secuencia. El undercut funcionó, ya que NOR terminó P3 por delante de PIA (P4).

GAS realizó un overcut a BEA al parar en vuelta 55 (bajo SC/VSC) mientras BEA paró en vuelta 54 (bajo SC/VSC), según los datos de pit-timing. 

Esto mantuvo a GAS una vuelta más en pista antes de montar el stint final de SOFT. 

El gap GAS→BEA pasó de 4.73 s a 54.525 s tras esas paradas. 

El overcut funcionó, ya que el gap se amplió drásticamente a favor de GAS.

ALO ran a 4-stint strategy: SOFT (laps 1-15, avg 83.10 s, deg +0.274 s/lap), MEDIUM (laps 16-42, avg 81.59 s, deg +0.035 s/lap), SOFT (laps 43-54, avg 80.36 s, deg +0.202 s/lap), and SOFT (laps 55-66, avg 79.29 s, deg -0.473 s/lap), pitting at laps 15, 42 and 54 (the final stop under SC/VSC).

The long MEDIUM stint delivered the lowest degradation rate (+0.035 s/lap) and a lower average time than the opening SOFT stint. The two final SOFT stints produced successively quicker averages (80.36 s then 79.29 s), with the last stint also showing negative degradation. The SC/VSC pit at lap 54 enabled the final tire change with no added time loss, supporting the improved pace that yielded P9.

HUL used a 4-stint strategy: Soft (laps 1-9, avg 82.11s, deg -0.047s/lap) to Medium at pit lap 9, Medium (laps 10-45, avg 81.72s, deg -0.024s/lap) to Soft at pit lap 45, Soft (laps 46-56, avg 80.45s, deg -0.051s/lap) to Soft at pit lap 56 under SC/VSC, then Soft (laps 57-66, avg 78.34s, deg -0.065s/lap).

This produced P5 via the Medium stint's lowest degradation enabling a long, consistent run with avg only 0.39s faster than the opening Soft, plus progressively quicker Soft avgs (80.45s then 78.34s) after the SC/VSC stop.

RUS empleó una estrategia de 4 stints: Soft (laps 1-20, media 81.74 s, deg +0.048 s/lap), pit lap 20 a Medium (laps 21-41, media 80.37 s, deg +0.048 s/lap), pit lap 41 a Soft (laps 42-55, media 79.23 s, deg +0.060 s/lap) y pit lap 55 a Soft bajo SC/VSC (laps 56-66, media 78.13 s, deg +0.038 s/lap).

Las medias de vuelta descendieron progresivamente (81.74 → 80.37 → 79.23 → 78.13 s). El stint final mostró la menor degradación (+0.038 s/lap). El pit bajo SC/VSC en lap 55 limitó la pérdida de tiempo en el cambio final. Esta secuencia permitió completar las 66 vueltas y terminar P4.

ALO undercut a LAW al parar en vuelta 42 (de MEDIUM a SOFT), dos vueltas antes que LAW (vuelta 44, de MEDIUM a SOFT). 

En las vueltas 43-44, ALO rodó en SOFT fresco (stint 3: laps 43-54) mientras LAW aún estaba en MEDIUM (stint 2 hasta lap 44). Esto generó el undercut. 

El gap ALO→LAW pasó de -4.833 s a -1.049 s tras esas paradas, confirmando que ALO ganó tiempo relativo. 

Funcionó: el gap se redujo 3.784 s y ALO acabó P9 por delante de LAW (P11).

BOR undercut OCO al parar en vuelta 19 (SOFT→MEDIUM), un lap antes que OCO (vuelta 20, SOFT→MEDIUM), según los pit laps indicados.  

Tras esa diferencia de timing, el gap BOR→OCO aumentó de 1.593 s a 2.768 s.  

Ese incremento del gap confirma que el undercut funcionó, al permitir a BOR rodar en MEDIUM fresco mientras OCO aún completaba su última vuelta en SOFT.

OCO undercut a TSU al parar en la vuelta 43 (MEDIUM→MEDIUM), un giro antes que TSU (SOFT→SOFT en vuelta 44).  

Antes de esas paradas el gap OCO→TSU era -2.208 s; tras ellas pasó a +0.933 s.  

Esto confirma que la parada temprana de OCO generó suficiente ganancia en el out-lap para invertir el orden. El undercut funcionó en ese momento según el cambio de gap registrado.

TSU paró en la vuelta 54 (stint 4 SOFT finalizado) y SAI en la 55 (stint 3 MEDIUM finalizado), ambos bajo SC/VSC.  

El gap TSU→SAI pasó de -0.582 s a 3.239 s tras estas paradas.  

Este timing de una vuelta antes permitió a TSU ganar la posición sobre SAI. Funcionó, ya que TSU terminó P13 y SAI P14.

SAI ejecutó el overcut al permanecer en pista hasta la vuelta 38 (stint 2 de 21 vueltas en MEDIUM), mientras HAD paró en la vuelta 37 (stint 2 de solo 19 vueltas en MEDIUM).  

Tras estas paradas, el gap SAI→HAD pasó de -4.033 s a -2.756 s, lo que muestra que el timing de SAI le permitió ganar 1.277 s netos respecto a HAD.  

El overcut funcionó en términos de reducción de distancia, aunque SAI terminó P13 y HAD P8.

HAD utilizó una estrategia de tres stints con dos paradas. Comenzó con SOFT en las vueltas 1-18 (18 vueltas, promedio 75.50 s, degradación +0.203 s/vuelta), cambió a MEDIUM en el pit de la vuelta 18 y completó 19 vueltas (19-37) con promedio 75.01 s y degradación +0.054 s/vuelta. En el pit de la vuelta 37 montó otro juego de MEDIUM para cubrir las vueltas 38-71 (34 vueltas, promedio 74.74 s, degradación +0.032 s/vuelta).

Esta secuencia redujo progresivamente la degradación (de +0.203 a +0.032 s/vuelta) y mejoró el ritmo medio en cada stint (75.50 → 75.01 → 74.74 s), permitiendo completar las 71 vueltas y finalizar en P8.

La estrategia de PIA fue de dos paradas: stint 1 en MEDIUM (vueltas 1-38, avg 75.02 s, deg +0.025 s/vuelta), cambio a SOFT en vuelta 38 para stint 2 (vueltas 39-51, 13 vueltas, avg 73.62 s, deg +0.030 s/vuelta), y regreso a MEDIUM en vuelta 51 para stint 3 (vueltas 52-71, 20 vueltas, avg 73.33 s, deg +0.041 s/vuelta).

Esto influyó en el resultado al reducir el avg de vuelta de 75.02 s (stint 1) a 73.62 s (stint 2) y 73.33 s (stint 3), mejorando el ritmo en las últimas 33 vueltas y permitiendo finalizar en P5.

ALB attempted the undercut by pitting on lap 35 (MEDIUM to SOFT) while HAD stayed out until lap 37 (MEDIUM to MEDIUM). This placed ALB on fresh SOFT tires for laps 36-37 against HAD's older MEDIUM tires.  

The provided pit-timing data shows the ALB-to-HAD gap then widened from 4.204 s to 5.876 s.  

The undercut therefore did not succeed in closing the gap or changing track position.

BEA undercut a HAD al parar en la vuelta 17 (un giro antes que HAD en la 18). 

Según los datos, el gap BEA→HAD pasó de -1.287 s a +3.508 s tras esa secuencia. Esto muestra que BEA ganó la posición en pista al montar el Medium fresco antes y rodar con él mientras HAD aún estaba en Soft degradado.

Funcionó: BEA terminó P6 y HAD P8, con el gap ya positivo a favor de BEA.

HAD ejecutó el undercut parando en la vuelta 37 (dos vueltas antes que GAS en la 39).  

Durante las vueltas 38-39, HAD rodó con medium fresco (stint 3 iniciado en vuelta 38, avg 74.74 s) mientras GAS aún usaba su medium de stint 2 (21 vueltas acumuladas hasta la 39, avg 75.04 s).  

Tras las dos paradas, el gap HAD→GAS pasó de 0.697 s a 2.455 s.  

La maniobra funcionó: HAD terminó P8 y GAS P10.

SAI paró en vuelta 17 (SOFT→MEDIUM) mientras LAW siguió en SOFT hasta la vuelta 19.  

Los datos del pit-timing battle muestran que el gap SAI→LAW pasó de -2.302 s a -0.794 s tras estas paradas. Esto confirma que el undercut de dos vueltas redujo la diferencia en 1.508 s.  

El undercut funcionó en reducción de gap, pero no en posición final (SAI P13, LAW P7).

HUL overcut BEA by staying out 3 laps longer on mediums (pit lap 33 vs. BEA pit lap 30), extending his first stint to 33 laps while BEA stopped after 30.  

Data shows this timing directly improved the HUL-to-BEA gap from -1.685 s to -0.387 s after both had pitted (HUL then running 23 laps on softs at 99.39 s avg; BEA 26 laps at 99.71 s avg).  

The strategy worked: HUL finished P8 ahead of BEA’s P9.

HAM utilizó una estrategia de una parada: stint 1 con MEDIUM en las vueltas 1-31 (31 vueltas, media 100.30 s, degradación +0.001 s/vuelta) seguido de parada en la vuelta 31 para montar SOFT en el stint 2 (vueltas 32-56, 25 vueltas, media 99.04 s, degradación +0.062 s/vuelta). 

El primer stint ofreció ritmo estable por la mínima degradación indicada. El cambio a SOFT redujo la media de vuelta en 1.26 s respecto al MEDIUM, aunque con mayor degradación, lo que sostuvo un ritmo más rápido en las últimas 25 vueltas y permitió acabar en P4.

La estrategia de PIA consistió en una parada única: 30 vueltas en MEDIUM (laps 1-30, avg 100.44 s, deg -0.000 s/vuelta) y cambio en pit lap 30 a SOFT para las 26 vueltas finales (laps 31-56, avg 98.91 s, deg -0.023 s/vuelta).  

El primer stint mostró ritmo estable sin degradación. El segundo stint redujo el promedio de vuelta en 1.53 s respecto al primero, lo que permitió finalizar en P5.

COL undercut BOR al parar en la vuelta 32 (MEDIUM→SOFT) mientras BOR seguía en MEDIUM hasta su parada en la vuelta 36.  

Durante las vueltas 33-36, COL rodó con SOFT fresco (promedio stint 2: 100.04 s) frente al MEDIUM de BOR (promedio stint 2: 100.84 s).  

El gap COL→BOR pasó de 0.835 s a 6.143 s tras las paradas.  

Funcionó: COL terminó P17 y BOR P18.

PIA paró en vuelta 30 (MEDIUM→SOFT) mientras RUS continuó hasta vuelta 33 (MEDIUM→SOFT), según los stints provistos.  
El gap PIA→RUS pasó de 1.324 s a 4.016 s en ese intervalo de timing.  
Esto demuestra que el undercut funcionó, ya que PIA finalizó P5 por delante de RUS en P6.

ALB executou overcut ao estender stint 2 em HARD até o lap 33 (25 voltas totais), pitando uma volta depois de ANT (lap 32). 

Isso é diretamente indicado pelos dados de pit-timing: ALB pitted lap 33 vs ANT lap 32, alterando gap ALB->ANT de -2.902s para -2.312s. 

O overcut não produziu inversão de posição, pois ALB terminou P16 e ANT P15.

HUL used a two-stop strategy: Softs for laps 1-7 (avg 90.79s, deg -0.285s/lap), pitting at lap 7 to Hards for laps 8-42 (35 laps, avg 90.01s, deg +0.029s/lap), then pitting at lap 42 to Mediums for laps 43-58 (16 laps, avg 88.09s, deg +0.101s/lap).

The long Hard stint delivered consistent pace with near-zero degradation. The final Medium stint produced the lowest average lap time of the race. This sequence completed all 58 laps and yielded a P9 finish.

PIA used a one-stop strategy: 41 laps on HARD (avg 89.41 s, deg -0.018 s/lap) followed by a pit on lap 41 to MEDIUM for laps 42-58 (avg 87.44 s, deg +0.081 s/lap). The long HARD stint provided stable pace over the majority of the 58-lap distance, while the late switch delivered a faster average on MEDIUM, enabling the P2 finish.

BEA pitted on lap 14 (MEDIUM to HARD) while SAI stayed out until lap 18. This placed BEA on fresh HARD tires (stint avg 89.64 s) for laps 15–18 against SAI’s aging MEDIUM tires (stint avg 90.80 s, +0.042 s/lap degradation).  

The provided gap data shows BEA-to-SAI increased from 0.811 s to 7.067 s after these stops. BEA finished P12, SAI P13. The earlier stop therefore produced a lasting position gain.

HAD attempted the undercut by pitting lap 14 (4 laps before SAI's lap 18 stop). This placed HAD on fresh HARD tires for laps 15-18 while SAI stayed on worn MEDIUMs. The provided pit-timing data shows the gap HAD→SAI widened from 4.686 s to 9.108 s after the sequence. HAD's HARD stint averaged 90.09 s versus SAI's 89.62 s, and HAD finished P17 behind SAI's P13. The undercut therefore did not gain position.

OCO realizou o undercut ao parar no pit na volta 19 (troca MEDIUM→HARD), dois giros antes de LAW (volta 21, mesma troca).

Isso é baseado diretamente nos dados de pit lap de cada piloto. Enquanto OCO já rodava no HARD fresco nas voltas 20-21, LAW ainda estava no MEDIUM (stint 1 com degradação +0.078 s/lap). O resultado do timing aparece no gap OCO→LAW, que passou de 4.3 s para 21.214 s.

A manobra funcionou: OCO completou o stint 2 no HARD com média 89.41 s (39 voltas, degradação -0.018 s/lap) e terminou P7, contra média 89.53 s de LAW (36 voltas) e P18 final.

RUS undercut NOR by pitting 2 laps earlier (lap 14 vs. NOR's lap 16).  
This is shown directly by RUS stint 1 ending after 14 medium laps with pit lap 14, while NOR ran 16 medium laps before pitting on lap 16.  

The timing produced a gap change from -4.776 s to -3.301 s.  
This reduced NOR's lead by 1.475 s, confirming the undercut gained time on fresher hard tires during NOR's final 2 medium laps.  

It partially succeeded in closing the gap but did not reverse the order, as evidenced by final positions (NOR P3, RUS P5) and NOR's additional stop on lap 40.

ALB overcut GAS via pit timing on laps 2-3: ALB pitted lap 3 (INTERMEDIATE->INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC) while GAS pitted lap 2 (same compound change under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this later stop extended the ALB->GAS gap from 1.919s to 3.96s. The overcut worked, as shown by the widened gap on the provided timing data.

ALO attempted an overcut by remaining on track one lap longer than GAS, pitting lap 3 versus GAS pit lap 2 (both under SC/VSC, INTERMEDIATE-to-INTERMEDIATE). 

Per the pit-timing battle data, this timing directly produced a gap change from -3.06 s to -1.714 s. 

The overcut therefore succeeded in reducing ALO’s deficit to GAS by 1.346 s on that exchange. 

Both drivers then ran identical INTERMEDIATE stints laps 5-33 (29 laps, 92.50 s average), confirming the early timing gain carried forward without further offset in the supplied data.

ANT overcut OCO via later pit timing: data shows ANT pitted lap 3 vs OCO pit lap 2.  

Post-stop gap ANT-to-OCO widened from 3.383 s to 11.446 s. This timing directly produced the larger delta shown.  

The overcut worked per the recorded gap increase.

BOR attempted the overcut by pitting one lap later than LAW (lap 3 vs. lap 2). This is directly stated in the pit-timing battle data for the early SC/VSC phase.

The timing produced a larger gap: BOR→LAW expanded from 4.713 s to 10.154 s immediately after the stops. No other laps or gaps are provided for this window.

The overcut did not yield track-position gain. Both drivers remained on INTERMEDIATE through lap 33 with no position swap shown, and BOR ultimately finished P16 behind LAW’s P15.

GAS overcut ALB by pitting one lap later (lap 4 vs. ALB lap 3, both under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this changed the gap GAS→ALB from -4.348 s to -3.236 s. The later timing therefore narrowed the deficit by 1.112 s. No other pit-timing gap data is provided, so the only measurable effect is this early gap reduction.

HAM overcut GAS by pitting later (lap 3 vs. GAS pit on lap 2).  
Data shows the HAM-to-GAS gap increased from 1.123 s to 2.219 s immediately after.  

This timing produced the final result of HAM P10 ahead of GAS P11.

HUL overcut ANT via later pit timing on lap 4 (vs. ANT's pit on lap 2), per the pit-timing battle data. Both ran identical INTERMEDIATE stints 1-4 up to that point under SC/VSC.  

Post-pit, the HUL-to-ANT gap widened from 1.085 s to 1.631 s.  

The overcut did not work: HUL finished P7, ANT P4.

HUL attempted the overcut on STR via pit timing by staying out one lap longer (HUL pitted lap 4; STR pitted lap 3). 

Per the provided pit-timing battle data, this changed the gap HUL→STR from -1.666 s to -0.853 s.

LAW overcut OCO via the lap-3 vs lap-2 pit timing under SC/VSC. Data shows LAW remained on track one lap longer than OCO, after which the LAW-to-OCO gap narrowed from -3.093 s to -0.877 s.  

This timing therefore reduced the deficit by 2.216 s. The overcut succeeded on gap closure but did not produce a final position gain (OCO P13, LAW P15). No other pit timings or gaps are provided.

LEC overcut ALB by extending stint 4 on INTERMEDIATE to lap 34 before pitting (INTERMEDIATE→HARD under SC/VSC), while ALB ended stint 4 on lap 33 (INTERMEDIATE→MEDIUM under SC/VSC). This is confirmed by LEC's 30-lap INTERMEDIATE stint (laps 5-34, avg 91.77s) versus ALB's 29-lap INTERMEDIATE stint (laps 5-33, avg 92.41s).

Post-pit, LEC ran HARD laps 35-47 (13 laps, avg 89.51s, +2.704s/lap deg) before another stop at lap 47, while ALB ran MEDIUM laps 34-44 (11 laps, avg 85.86s) before stopping at lap 44. The early pit-timing data (LEC stop lap 4 vs ALB lap 2) shows gap LEC→ALB widening from 3.646s to 4.774s after LEC's later stop, with no counter-data indicating position gain from the lap-34 overcut.

De acordo com os dados, LEC realizou overcut em RUS ao pitpar na volta 4 (enquanto RUS pitou na volta 2), conforme indicado na seção pit-timing battle. Isso alterou o gap LEC→RUS de -3.846 s para -2.847 s.

O overcut funcionou, pois reduziu o déficit de tempo em 0.999 s.

Não há dados de gap para o pit de volta 47 de LEC vs. volta 44 de RUS.

OCO overcut BEA via later pit timing on lap 3 (vs BEA's lap 2). Per the pit-timing battle data, this extended OCO's stint and widened the gap OCO→BEA from 3.554 s to 7.383 s.  

The overcut worked: OCO finished P13 ahead of BEA in P14.

RUS overcut PIA by pitting on lap 4 while PIA pitted on lap 2. The provided pit-timing battle data shows the gap RUS→PIA changed from -3.85s to -1.889s as a direct result of this later stop.  

This timing produced a net gain for RUS relative to PIA. The overcut therefore succeeded on the evidence of the gap reduction alone. Both drivers later used identical pit laps 34 and 44, so the only timing difference was the lap-4 versus lap-2 choice.

STR overcut ALO by pitting one lap later (lap 4 vs. lap 3).  
Pit-timing battle data shows this directly reduced the gap STR→ALO from -2.362 s to -1.366 s.  
The timing gain is therefore +0.996 s and is the only lap-specific delta provided.

TSU overcut GAS by pitting one lap later: data shows TSU pitted lap 3 (INTERMEDIATE) vs GAS on lap 2. After these stops the gap TSU→GAS widened from 3.464 s to 5.934 s, confirming the later stop extended the advantage. No other timing difference is given.

TSU overcut LEC via later pit timing on lap 4 (vs LEC's pit on lap 3), per the pit-timing battle data; both stops occurred under SC/VSC during the lap-4 INTERMEDIATE stint.  

This is directly shown by TSU's stint 3 (laps 1-4 sequence ending in pit lap 4) versus LEC's pit lap 3.  

Post-stop, the gap TSU→LEC changed from -3.264 s to -2.157 s. The data shows the overcut narrowed the gap but provides no further position or outcome details.

HAM realizou 5 pit stops, todos sob SC/VSC. 

Stints iniciais curtos em INTERMEDIATE (laps 1-2, 3 e 4) com trocas em 2, 3 e 4. Stint longo em INTERMEDIATE (laps 5-33, 29 voltas, média 92.44 s, deg -0.248 s/volta). Troca para HARD no pit 33, stint de 14 voltas (34-47, média 89.48 s, deg +2.732 s/volta). Retorno a INTERMEDIATE no pit 47 para as 10 voltas finais (48-57, média 92.29 s, deg -0.954 s/volta). 

O HARD entregou a menor média de tempo, mas a degradação elevada forçou a troca final; as paradas sob SC/VSC eliminaram perda de tempo e permitiram completar as 57 voltas em P10.

PIA's strategy used six stints with five pit stops, all under SC/VSC except the final change:

- Early INTERMEDIATE stints (laps 1-4) involved three short changes (2+1+1 laps) via pits at laps 2, 3 and 4.
- Long INTERMEDIATE stint 4 ran laps 5-34 (30 laps, 90.39 s average, -0.228 s/lap degradation).
- Switch to HARD at pit lap 34 covered laps 35-44 (10 laps, 83.91 s average).

These SC/VSC-timed changes enabled the full 57-lap distance on the listed compounds, resulting in P9.

ALB pitted lap 2 (INTERMEDIATE->INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC) while ALO pitted lap 3 (same compound change under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this earlier stop timing produced a gap change from 4.979 s to 5.674 s (ALB ahead). The undercut therefore worked by extending the gap.

ANT undercut LEC by pitting on lap 44 (HARD to INTERMEDIATE) while LEC stayed out until lap 47.

ANT then completed 13 laps on fresh INTERMEDIATE (45-57, avg 90.74s, deg -0.266s/lap) while LEC did only 10 laps on the same compound (48-57, avg 91.82s).

BEA undercut OCO via earlier pit timing on lap 2 versus OCO on lap 4.

The undercut worked in closing the time deficit; no other outcomes are stated in the data.

GAS pitted lap 3 (INTERMEDIATE→INTERMEDIATE) while HAM pitted lap 4, per the pit-timing battle data.  

This timing produced a gap change from -2.636 s to -1.807 s (GAS→HAM).

HAM pitted lap 2 (INTERMEDIATE) while GAS pitted lap 3 (INTERMEDIATE), both under SC/VSC. Per the pit-timing battle data, this one-lap-earlier stop widened the HAM-to-GAS gap from 1.123 s to 2.219 s.  

The earlier timing therefore produced an undercut. It left HAM with a larger advantage on track immediately afterward.

HUL pitted on lap 2 (INTERMEDIATE to INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC) while ANT stayed out until lap 4. Per the pit-timing data, this earlier stop increased the HUL-to-ANT gap from 1.085 s to 1.631 s.  

The undercut therefore did not close the gap or produce a net gain.

HUL pitted lap 44 (MEDIUM→INTERMEDIATE), while LEC stayed out until lap 47 (HARD→INTERMEDIATE).  

During LEC’s extended HARD stint (laps 35-47), tire degradation reached +2.704 s/lap. HUL’s preceding MEDIUM stint (laps 34-44) posted a faster average (86.36 s).  

Post-stop, the HUL→LEC gap reversed from –4.984 s to +7.306 s. HUL finished P7, LEC P8.  

The earlier stop therefore produced a net track-position gain for HUL.

LAW undercut BEA via pit timing on lap 2 (vs. BEA lap 3). Data shows gap LAW->BEA changed from 0.461s to 6.506s after these stops (both under SC/VSC). 

This timing produced the documented gap increase. It did not yield a net position gain, as LAW finished P15 and BEA P14.

LAW pitted lap 2 while OCO remained out until pit lap 4. Per the pit-timing battle data, this earlier stop changed the gap LAW→OCO from -3.093 s to -1.642 s (net gain of 1.451 s for LAW). The undercut therefore succeeded in closing the gap.

The later stops (LAW pit lap 33 vs OCO pit lap 39) show no post-stop gap data; final classification was LAW P15 / OCO P13.

LEC pitted one lap earlier than HAM (lap 2 vs lap 3, both INTERMEDIATE->INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this timing extended the LEC->HAM gap from 4.442s to 7.028s. The undercut therefore worked, as the gap increased.

LEC pitted lap 34 (INTERMEDIATE to HARD) while TSU pitted lap 33 (INTERMEDIATE to MEDIUM).  

LEC then ran 13 laps on HARD (laps 35-47) at 89.51 s average with +2.704 s/lap degradation. TSU ran 14 laps on MEDIUM (laps 34-47) at 90.77 s average with +3.951 s/lap degradation.  

No post-pit gap, position or delta data is given for laps 33-47, so the data does not confirm whether track-position or time advantage was gained. The only pit-timing gap provided (LEC lap 2 vs TSU lap 3) shows the gap widening from 2.101 s to 3.313 s when LEC stopped one lap earlier.
OCO pitted on lap 2 vs BEA on lap 3. Per the pit-timing battle data, this timing changed the OCO-to-BEA gap from 3.554 s to 7.383 s. The larger gap shows the undercut succeeded on the available data.

RUS pitted lap 44 (HARD to INTERMEDIATE), while HAM stayed out until pitting lap 47 for the same stop. This 3-lap earlier timing by RUS created the undercut window versus HAM.  

RUS's preceding HARD stint ran only laps 35-44 (10 laps at 85.65 s avg). HAM's HARD stint ran laps 34-47 (14 laps at 89.48 s avg with +2.732 s/lap degradation).  

The RUS-to-HAM gap therefore moved from 2.366 s to 11.485 s. The timing produced a net gain for RUS.

RUS pitted on lap 3 while PIA pitted on lap 4. Per the pit-timing battle data, this changed the gap RUS->PIA from -2.453s to -1.889s. The earlier stop therefore narrowed the interval between them. The undercut succeeded on the metric supplied in the data.

STR pitted lap 2 while ALO pitted lap 4 (both under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this changed the STR→ALO gap from -2.265 s to -1.366 s.  

The earlier stop timing therefore reduced STR’s deficit to ALO by 0.899 s with no extra track time lost.  

It worked on that metric; the data records no reversal of the gap improvement from those stops.

STR realizou undercut em GAS parando na volta 44 (fim do stint HARD laps 34-44), enquanto GAS permaneceu até a volta 46 (stint HARD laps 34-46). 

O pit-timing battle mostra o gap STR→GAS mudando de -1.774s para 2.303s após esses stops. 

Essa alteração de gap confirma que o undercut funcionou, com STR terminando à frente (P6 vs P11 de GAS).

TSU pitted lap 2 while ALB pitted lap 4 (both INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC). This earlier stop by TSU was an undercut attempt.  

After these stops the gap TSU→ALB widened from 1.545 s to 2.617 s. The undercut therefore did not succeed.

TSU pitted on lap 2 (INTERMEDIATE->INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC) while HAM stayed out until pitting on lap 4 (INTERMEDIATE->INTERMEDIATE under SC/VSC).  

Per the pit-timing battle data, this earlier stop changed the gap TSU->HAM from 2.341 s to 4.046 s.  

The data records no position change or overtaking outcome from this gap increase, so the undercut produced a larger time delta but no demonstrated net gain.

HAD overcut STR via later pit timing: HAD switched MED->HARD on lap 28 while STR switched MED->HARD on lap 26, staying out exactly two laps longer. During this window the HAD->STR gap grew from 2.125 s to 3.178 s.  

HAD therefore rejoined with track-position advantage on the new HARD stint. The overcut succeeded, as HAD finished P12 and STR finished P14.

HUL adotou estratégia de 2 paradas: stint 1 de 12 voltas em SOFT (média 71.56s, deg -0.149s/lap), pit volta 12 para MEDIUM; stint 2 de 30 voltas (13-42) em MEDIUM (média 71.02s, deg -0.002s/lap), pit volta 42 para MEDIUM; stint 3 de 27 voltas (43-69) em MEDIUM (média 70.27s, deg -0.018s/lap).

A degradação quase nula dos médios versus o SOFT permitiu médias progressivamente mais baixas e ritmo estável, resultando em P9.

OCO adotou estratégia de duas paradas: stint inicial de 19 voltas em MEDIUM (média 71,68 s, deg +0,008 s/volta), troca para HARD na volta 19, stint de 31 voltas em HARD (média 70,81 s, deg -0,003 s/volta), nova troca para HARD na volta 50 e stint final de 19 voltas (média 70,02 s, deg +0,036 s/volta).  

O segundo stint longo em HARD reduziu a média em 0,87 s em relação ao primeiro e manteve degradação quase nula, sustentando ritmo competitivo. O terceiro stint, apesar da degradação mais alta, entregou a média mais rápida (70,02 s). Essa combinação de stints em HARD permitiu completar as 69 voltas registradas e chegar em P10.

BEA undercut GAS via earlier pit timing, stopping on lap 11 (ending 11-lap SOFT stint) while GAS stopped on lap 12 (ending 12-lap SOFT stint). Per the pit-timing battle data, this changed the BEA->GAS gap from -2.563s to +0.678s. The undercut worked, as BEA finished P11 ahead of GAS in P13.

NOR undercut PIA by pitting on lap 20 (MEDIUM→HARD) while PIA remained out until lap 24 (MEDIUM→HARD). This gave NOR fresh HARD tires on laps 21–24 (part of his 32-lap HARD stint at 69.80 s avg) against PIA’s aging MEDIUM stint 1 (24 laps at 70.45 s avg). Per the pit-timing data, the NOR→PIA gap grew from 0.665 s to 6.28 s. The undercut worked, as NOR finished P1 and PIA P2.

HAD executou overcut em BOR ao pitlar na volta 29 (após stint 1 de 29 voltas em MEDIUM), enquanto BOR pitlou na volta 26 (fim do stint 1 de 26 voltas em HARD).  

O timing gerou aumento do gap HAD→BOR de 3.76 s para 5.714 s.  

Funcionou: HAD terminou P10, BOR P11.

LAW used a one-stop strategy, pitting on lap 20 from MEDIUM to HARD. The 20-lap MEDIUM stint (laps 1-20) posted an average of 106.93s with -0.154s/lap degradation. The subsequent 31-lap HARD stint (laps 21-51) delivered a faster average of 105.30s and far lower degradation of -0.048s/lap. The HARD compound's superior longevity and pace over the longer stint supported consistent running that secured P5.

SAI adotou estratégia de uma parada: iniciou no Medium (voltas 1-27, média 106,39 s, degradação -0,080 s/volta) e trocou no pit da volta 27 para o Hard (voltas 28-51, média 104,77 s, degradação -0,047 s/volta).  

O Medium apresentou média mais lenta e degradação mais acentuada nos primeiros 27 giros; o Hard entregou média 1,62 s mais rápida e degradação 41 % menor nas 24 voltas finais. Essa troca permitiu ritmo sustentado na segunda metade da prova, culminando em P3.

ANT undercut LAW by pitting lap 18 (MEDIUM→HARD) while LAW stayed out until lap 20. This put ANT on fresh HARD tires for laps 19-20 (stint-2 avg 105.00 s) against LAW’s used MEDIUM tires (stint-1 avg 106.93 s).  

The timing flipped the gap ANT→LAW from –0.463 s to +0.194 s. ANT therefore gained track position and finished P4 ahead of LAW in P5.

ALO overcut HAD by pitting 4 laps later (lap 32 vs. lap 28). Per the pit-timing data, this changed the ALO-to-HAD gap from -1.712 s to +3.562 s, confirming ALO emerged ahead after the stops. The overcut therefore succeeded on timing and gap swing alone. (Final positions P15 vs. P13 are outside this specific battle and not used here.)

(The SC/VSC on ALO’s lap-32 stop further enabled the later timing without time loss.)

SAI attempted an overcut on DOO by extending Stint 2 to lap 32 on medium (18 laps, avg 98.71s) while DOO ended Stint 2 at lap 28 on medium (19 laps, avg 98.83s). SAI's pit occurred at lap 32 (medium to hard under SC/VSC) versus DOO's pit at lap 28 (medium to hard). Per the pit-timing data, the SAI-to-DOO gap changed from -2.215s to -0.784s after these stops. This narrowed the gap by 1.431s. The overcut therefore closed relative time but did not produce a final position gain (SAI P19, DOO P14).

LEC usou estratégia de 3 stints: Medium (1-17), Medium (18-32) e Hard (33-57). 

A primeira troca (lap 17, M→M) ocorreu após 17 voltas com média 98,77 s e degradação +0,053 s/lap. 

A segunda troca (lap 32, M→H sob SC/VSC) permitiu stint final de 25 voltas com média mais baixa (96,98 s), apesar de degradação mais alta (+0,099 s/lap). 

Os tempos médios decrescentes (98,77 s → 97,61 s → 96,98 s) e o pit sob SC/VSC sustentaram ritmo competitivo até a volta 57, resultando em P4.

VER executou o overcut ao pit-stopar uma volta depois de LEC (volta 23 vs. volta 22), conforme os dados de pit-timing battle.

A estratégia de HAD foi de três stints com duas paradas sob SC/VSC: stint 1 em Medium (voltas 1-23, média 76,17 s, deg +0,025 s/volta), stint 2 em Hard (voltas 24-53, média 75,06 s, deg -0,013 s/volta) e stint 3 em Soft (voltas 54-72, média 73,67 s, deg -0,037 s/volta).

Stint 2 em HARD (voltas 9-52, 44 voltas, média 76.29s, deg +0.017s/lap). Pit na volta 52 para novo HARD. Stint 3 em HARD (voltas 53-72, 20 voltas, média 75.14s, deg -0.102s/lap). Essa estratégia permitiu completar as 72 voltas, terminando em P7.

ALB undercut SAI via pit timing because ALB stopped lap 23 (MED→HARD under SC/VSC) while SAI’s corresponding stop was lap 27 (HARD→SOFT after only 4 laps on that set).  

The data directly records the result of this 4-lap offset: gap ALB→SAI changed from –2.123 s to +76.925 s.  

This timing worked, as ALB finished P5 and SAI finished P13.

COL pitted lap 19 (SOFT→HARD) while BOR remained out until lap 22 (MEDIUM→HARD). During laps 20-22 COL therefore ran new HARD tires against BOR’s older MEDIUM tires. The gap COL→BOR widened from 4.412 s to 7.508 s across this window. The undercut succeeded on timing alone, producing a net gain of ~3.1 s on BOR.

LEC attempted an overcut by staying out one lap longer on MEDIUM, pitting lap 10 (after 10 laps at avg 81.86 s) versus GAS pitting lap 9 (after 9 laps at avg 82.56 s).  

The provided pit-timing data shows this timing extended the gap LEC→GAS from 4.407 s to 6.537 s. Both then ran identical second stints (HARD to lap 29) and pitted together under SC/VSC.  

The overcut therefore succeeded in increasing the gap as recorded.

LEC adotou estratégia de duas paradas: stint inicial de 10 voltas em Medium (voltas 1-10, média 81.86 s, degradação -0.059 s/volta), troca para Hard na volta 10; stint seguinte de 19 voltas em Hard (voltas 11-29, média 81.41 s, degradação +0.041 s/volta); segunda troca para Hard na volta 29 sob SC/VSC; stint final de 34 voltas em Hard (voltas 30-63, média 80.16 s, degradação +0.011 s/volta).  

A parada sob SC/VSC na volta 29 e o stint longo final em Hard com degradação baixa (+0.011 s/volta) e média mais rápida (80.16 s) permitiram completar as 63 voltas e chegar em P6.

SAI utilizou estratégia de dois pit stops: stint inicial em MEDIUM (voltas 1-11, média 81.91s, deg +0.072s/lap), troca na volta 11 para HARD, segundo stint em HARD (voltas 12-29, 18 voltas, média 81.88s, deg +0.076s/lap) e troca na volta 29 para HARD sob SC/VSC, completando stint final em HARD (voltas 30-63, 34 voltas, média 80.71s, deg -0.037s/lap).

A parada sob SC/VSC na volta 29 permitiu o stint mais longo sem penalidade de tempo significativa. Isso resultou na média mais baixa do GP (80.71s) com degradação negativa, inferior às médias anteriores de 81.91s e 81.88s, moldando a chegada em P8.

GAS realizou o undercut ao pitpar na volta 9 (MEDIUM→HARD), enquanto BOR permaneceu em MEDIUM até a volta 12 (pit MEDIUM→HARD).  

Os dados de pit-timing indicam que o gap GAS→BOR mudou de -0.917 s para +3.682 s após esses pit stops.  

O undercut funcionou, pois o gap passou de negativo (BOR à frente) para positivo (GAS à frente) pelo timing diferenciado.

LEC undercut ALO by pitting lap 10 (MED→HARD) versus ALO's lap 12 (MED→HARD). Per pit-timing data, this reversed the LEC→ALO gap from -3.0 s to +3.53 s. It worked, as the earlier switch placed LEC on fresh HARD (stint-2 avg 81.41 s) while ALO completed laps 11–12 on degrading MEDIUM (stint-1 avg 82.10 s, +0.175 s/lap deg).

LEC pitted on lap 10 (MEDIUM→HARD) while SAI remained on track until lap 11 (MEDIUM→HARD). This one-lap-earlier timing created the undercut window. After both stops the LEC→SAI gap reversed from −2.134 s to +3.031 s, placing LEC ahead. The undercut therefore succeeded on timing alone.

SAI realizou o undercut ao parar no giro 11 (MEDIUM→HARD), enquanto STR parou apenas no giro 14 (MEDIUM→HARD).  

Conforme os dados do pit-timing battle, o gap SAI→STR passou de 1.245 s para 4.959 s exatamente nesse intervalo de três giros.  

Isso ocorreu porque, nos giros 12-14, SAI já rodava no stint 2 de HARD (média 81.88 s) enquanto STR ainda completava o stint 1 de MEDIUM (média 82.17 s).  

O undercut funcionou: o gap ampliado se manteve e SAI terminou P8, STR P15.

HAM overcut HUL by pitting one lap later (lap 42 vs. lap 41), per the listed pit laps. HAM ran HARD to lap 42 then switched to MEDIUM; HUL had already switched MEDIUM-to-MEDIUM on lap 41.  

Post-pit gap HAM-to-HUL widened from 3.627 s to 9.795 s. HAM finished P12, HUL P13.

TSU executou overcut em COL ao permanecer em pista até a volta 37 (pit SOFT→HARD), enquanto COL parou na volta 35 (pit HARD→HARD).  

Antes do pit de TSU na volta 37, o gap TSU→COL era de -1.958 s (TSU atrás). Após o pit de TSU, o gap passou a +3.326 s (TSU à frente).  

Essa inversão de posição via timing mais tarde confirma que o overcut funcionou, conforme os dados de pit-timing fornecidos.

LAW utilizou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 de 40 voltas em MEDIUM (média 83.31 s, deg -0.026 s/lap) seguido de pit no lap 40 para HARD e stint 2 de 30 voltas (média 80.92 s, deg -0.006 s/lap).

Os dados indicam que o HARD entregou média 2.39 s mais rápida e degradação 4.33 vezes menor que o MEDIUM. Essa diferença de ritmo e consistência nas voltas 41-70 está associada à posição final P8.

RUS adotou estratégia de duas paradas: stint 1 de 19 voltas em MEDIUM (média 82.46 s, deg -0.064 s/volta), pit na volta 19 para HARD; stint 2 de 24 voltas em HARD (média 81.50 s, deg +0.008 s/volta), pit na volta 43 para novo HARD; stint final de 27 voltas em HARD (média 80.48 s, deg +0.042 s/volta). As médias de tempo por volta melhoraram progressivamente entre os três stints, alinhando-se ao resultado de P3.

BEA pitted on lap 30 (MEDIUM→HARD), two laps before GAS (lap 32, HARD→MEDIUM).  

During laps 31–32, GAS remained on his original HARD stint (32 laps total, avg 83.79 s) while BEA ran fresh HARD tires (stint avg 82.73 s).  

The BEA→GAS gap widened from 3.603 s to 6.386 s after the stops.  

Because the gap increased and BEA finished P20 behind GAS (P19), the earlier stop did not produce a net gain in track position.  

(The provided data contain no lap times or gaps that show BEA closing on GAS.)

STR pitted lap 36 (MEDIUM→HARD) while LAW stayed out until lap 40 on MEDIUM. This put STR on fresh HARD tires for laps 37-40, directly against LAW’s 36–40-lap-old MEDIUM stint.  

During that window the gap STR→LAW expanded from 0.555 s to 5.383 s. STR therefore gained track position via the undercut and finished P7 ahead of LAW’s P8.

BOR adotou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 em MEDIUM (laps 1-20, 20 voltas, média 84.54 s, deg -0.044 s/lap), pit no lap 20 para HARD, stint 2 (laps 21-53, 33 voltas, média 82.96 s, deg -0.051 s/lap).  

Essa estratégia cobriu as 53 voltas totais da prova, com média de volta mais baixa no stint longo em HARD (82.96 s) que no stint em MEDIUM (84.54 s), resultando em P8.

NOR usou estratégia de 1 parada: 46 voltas em MEDIUM (média 83.11 s, deg -0.033 s/vol) seguidas de pit no volta 46 para SOFT nas 7 voltas finais (média 81.13 s, deg -0.005 s/vol). 

A troca para SOFT reduziu a média de tempo em ~2 s por volta com degradação quase nula, sustentando ritmo competitivo até a bandeira e o P2 final.

ANT pitted lap 28 (MED→HARD) while SAI stayed out until lap 30 on MED. This put ANT on fresh HARD for laps 29-30 (stint-2 avg 82.62 s) against SAI’s final two MED laps (stint-1 avg 84.42 s). After both had pitted, the gap ANT→SAI widened from 4.81 s to 6.743 s. ANT therefore finished P9, SAI P11.

PIA pitted one lap earlier than NOR (lap 45 vs. lap 46), switching MEDIUM to SOFT while NOR remained on MEDIUM for lap 46.  

This is shown directly by PIA's stint-1 ending after 45 laps and stint-2 beginning on lap 46 (8 laps SOFT, avg 81.75 s), versus NOR's stint-1 extending to 46 laps before his SOFT stint (7 laps, avg 81.13 s).  

The provided pit-timing data records the PIA-to-NOR gap changing from -3.723 s to +2.075 s across these stops, confirming the earlier stop produced a net gain of ~5.8 s relative to NOR.  

The undercut therefore succeeded in closing and reversing the gap via tire freshness on the out-lap(s), but NOR still finished ahead (P2 vs. PIA P3).

ANT adotou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 em MEDIUM (voltas 1-31, média 93,67 s, degradação -0,046 s/volta), pit na volta 31 para HARD, stint 2 (voltas 32-53, média 91,39 s, degradação -0,026 s/volta). Com esses dados de stints e compostos, ANT terminou em P6.

LEC used a one-stop strategy: MEDIUM for laps 1-21 (avg 93.78s, deg -0.028s/lap), pitting lap 21 for HARD on laps 22-53 (avg 92.09s, deg -0.039s/lap). The HARD stint delivered the lower average lap time across the longer portion of the 53-lap race, producing the P4 result.

PIA pitted lap 20 (MEDIUM->HARD), one lap before NOR's pit on lap 21 (MEDIUM->HARD). This placed PIA on fresh HARD tires for lap 21 while NOR remained on MEDIUM tires for that lap. PIA's HARD stint (laps 21-53) posted 91.81 s average; NOR's MEDIUM stint (laps 1-21) posted 93.56 s average. Per the pit-timing data, this timing shifted the PIA-to-NOR gap from -2.52 s to -1.808 s. BEA overcut ALB via later pit timing: BEA stayed out until lap 17 on MEDIUM (17-lap stint, avg 97.78 s) before switching to HARD, while ALB pitted from HARD on lap 13. This produced a 4-lap offset in the pit-timing battle. Post-stops, the BEA-to-ALB gap widened from 4.525 s to 35.558 s. The later switch enabled BEA to finish P10, ahead of ALB in P16.

The timing produced a net gain: the ALO-to-BEA gap reversed from −1.832 s to +2.171 s after both had pitted.

TSU realizou o undercut ao fazer pit no lap 27 (HARD→MEDIUM), um lap antes do pit de LAW no lap 28 (HARD→HARD). Conforme os dados de pit-timing battle, esse timing fez o gap TSU→LAW subir de 2.789 s para 8.344 s. A manobra funcionou, pois ampliou a vantagem de TSU sobre LAW.

BEA adotou estratégia de dois pit stops: stint 1 em Soft (voltas 1-24, média 83,14s, degradação +0,013s/volta), stint 2 em Medium (voltas 25-48, média 82,42s, degradação -0,010s/volta) e stint 3 em Soft (voltas 49-71, média 81,10s, degradação +0,026s/volta), com trocas na volta 24 (Soft→Medium) e volta 48 (Medium→Soft).

Os dados mostram redução progressiva na média de tempo por volta (83,14s → 82,42s → 81,10s) e degradação negativa na stint de Medium, sustentando ritmo consistente que resultou em P4.

NOR adotou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 de 34 voltas em SOFT (média 82,57 s, degradação +0,019 s/volta), pit no lap 34 para MEDIUM e stint 2 de 37 voltas (média 81,53 s, degradação +0,007 s/volta) até o final das 71 voltas.

Essa escolha moldou o resultado ao reduzir o tempo médio em 1,04 s no stint mais longo e ao cortar a degradação em 63 % (+0,007 vs +0,019 s/volta), permitindo manter ritmo constante até a chegada em P1.

VER adotou estratégia de dois stints: 37 voltas em Medium (média 83,28 s, degradação +0,018 s/volta) até a parada na volta 37, trocando para Soft nas 34 voltas finais (média 81,34 s, degradação +0,003 s/volta).  

A troca para Soft reduziu a média de tempos em 1,94 s e praticamente eliminou a degradação, sustentando ritmo mais rápido e consistente no segundo stint, o que permitiu a chegada em P3.

ANT undercut RUS by pitting 3 laps earlier (lap 22 vs. lap 25), switching from SOFT to MEDIUM while RUS stayed on degrading SOFT.  

During laps 23-25 the provided gap ANT→RUS widened from 1.265 s to 4.664 s, confirming the time gain on fresh MEDIUM versus RUS’s worn SOFT (RUS stint-1 deg +0.018 s/lap).  

Both then ran MEDIUM stints; ANT’s lower deg (+0.003 s/lap) preserved the advantage through the next cycle, resulting in ANT finishing P6 ahead of RUS P7. The undercut therefore succeeded on the timing delta alone.

ANT's strategy used a short MEDIUM stint (laps 25-32: 8 laps, 91.82 s avg, +0.305 s/lap deg) then pitted lap 32 for HARD (laps 33-57: 25 laps, 91.16 s avg, -0.010 s/lap deg).  

The switch produced a 0.66 s lower average lap time and near-zero degradation on the longer final stint, enabling consistent pace to the P6 finish.

PIA realizou uma única parada no giro 32, trocando de MEDIUM para HARD.  

No stint de MEDIUM (voltas 25-32), completou 8 voltas com média de 91,07 s e degradação de -0,215 s/volta.  
No stint de HARD (voltas 33-57), completou as 25 voltas restantes com média de 90,32 s e degradação de +0,018 s/volta.  

A troca permitiu reduzir a média de tempo em 0,75 s por volta no composto mais durável, mantendo degradação quase nula até o fim da prova.

VER pitted on lap 32 (MEDIUM to HARD).  

The 8-lap MEDIUM stint (laps 25-32) produced 91.78 s average with -0.156 s/lap degradation.  

The 25-lap HARD stint (laps 33-57) produced 90.80 s average with +0.004 s/lap degradation.  

This switch delivered a lower average time and near-flat pace over the longer final stint, enabling the P4 finish.

HAD executou o undercut ao parar no lap 22 (MEDIUM→HARD), enquanto OCO parou apenas no lap 23 (MEDIUM→HARD).  

Isso é confirmado diretamente pelos dados de pit lap de cada um e pelo intervalo de uma volta entre as paradas.  

Durante o lap 23 de OCO ainda no MEDIUM (média 93,34 s), HAD já rodava no HARD novo (média da stint 91,45 s).  

O efeito aparece no gap HAD→OCO, que passou de -3,213 s para +1,11 s após as duas paradas.  

O undercut funcionou: HAD terminou à frente (P11 × P12).

TSU undercut HUL by pitting 4 laps earlier (lap 32 vs. lap 36).  

Data: TSU pit lap 32 (MED->HARD); HUL pit lap 36 (HARD->MED).  

The timing produced a net gain: gap TSU->HUL changed from -1.462 s to +21.297 s.  

This is confirmed by final classification (TSU P10, HUL P14). The undercut therefore succeeded.

HAM overcut OCO by extending stint 1 to lap 18 (HARD, 18 laps) while OCO pitted on lap 16 (MEDIUM, 16 laps).  

This timing is shown directly in the pit data (HAM pit lap 18 vs OCO pit lap 16) and produced the measured gap shift from 2.61 s to 8.043 s.  

The overcut succeeded: HAM finished P5, OCO finished P7.

HAM used a two-stop Hard-Hard-Medium strategy. Stint 1 on Hard (18 laps, avg 77.73 s, deg -0.515 s/lap) showed high degradation and required the lap-18 stop to Hard. Stint 2 on Hard (38 laps, avg 75.67 s, deg +0.010 s/lap) delivered lower average time with near-zero degradation. The lap-56 stop to Medium produced Stint 3 (22 laps, avg 75.85 s, deg -0.014 s/lap) with comparable pace stability. These stint averages and degradation values enabled consistent lap times across 78 laps, resulting in P5.

OCO usou estratégia de 2 paradas: Stint 1 Medium (voltas 1-16, média 78.48 s, deg -0.472 s/vol), pit volta 16 para Medium; Stint 2 Medium (voltas 17-28, média 77.20 s, deg +0.081 s/vol), pit volta 28 para Hard; Stint 3 Hard (voltas 29-77, média 77.20 s, deg +0.024 s/vol).

A troca precoce para Hard na volta 28 permitiu stint de 49 voltas com degradação mínima (+0.024 s/vol) e média idêntica à do Medium anterior (77.20 s), sustentando ritmo consistente até P7.

COL pitted lap 13 (HARD→MEDIUM) while BEA stayed out until lap 17 (HARD→MEDIUM).  
COL then ran fresh MEDIUM laps 14-26 at 77.55 s avg vs BEA’s HARD stint avg of 77.85 s.  
Pit-timing data shows gap COL→BEA widened from 2.188 s to 13.557 s.  
Undercut did not work: BEA finished P12, COL P13.

NOR undercut LEC ao pitlar na volta 19 (MEDIUM→HARD), enquanto LEC pitou apenas na volta 22 (MEDIUM→HARD).  

Os dados de pit-timing mostram que o gap NOR→LEC subiu de 3.07 s para 5.979 s após esse intervalo de 3 voltas.  

O aumento do gap confirma que o undercut funcionou, ampliando a vantagem de NOR.

ALO adotou estratégia de dois stops: stint inicial de 7 voltas em Medium (média 87,97 s, deg -0,125 s/volta), pit no lap 7 sob SC/VSC para novo Medium, seguido de 25 voltas (laps 8-32, média 87,12 s, deg -0,086 s/volta) e segundo pit no lap 32 para Hard nas 25 voltas finais (média 85,34 s, deg -0,096 s/volta).

O pit sob SC/VSC minimizou perda de tempo na primeira troca. O stint final em Hard entregou a menor média de volta (85,34 s) entre os três. Essa sequência permitiu completar as 57 voltas e chegar em P7.

NOR usou estratégia de 2 paradas: Medium (25 voltas), Medium (19 voltas) e Hard (13 voltas), com pits na volta 25 (Medium→Medium) e volta 44 (Medium→Hard).

Os dados mostram tempos médios decrescentes (86,26 s → 85,12 s → 83,80 s) e degradação reduzida no segundo Medium (-0,029 s/volta vs. -0,077 s/volta no primeiro), com o Hard apresentando pace mais rápido e degradação positiva (+0,038 s/volta). Isso sustentou o ritmo até o fim, resultando em P4.

TSU adotou estratégia de duas paradas (Medium–Medium–Hard).  
Stint 1 (Medium, voltas 1-7) registrou média 88,79 s e degradação elevada (-0,237 s/volta). Parada no lap 7 sob SC/VSC trocou Medium por Medium sem perda relevante de tempo.  
Stint 2 (Medium, voltas 8-32) apresentou degradação baixa (-0,040 s/volta) e média 87,59 s.  
Parada no lap 32 trocou para Hard. Stint 3 (Hard, voltas 33-57) entregou a melhor média da corrida (85,14 s) com degradação moderada (-0,083 s/volta), sustentando ritmo competitivo até a bandeira e permitindo chegada em P10.

BEA undercut OCO ao parar na volta 7 (MED→MED sob SC/VSC) enquanto OCO continuou até a volta 8 (MED→HARD sob SC/VSC).  

Isso é confirmado pelos pit laps e pelo pit-timing battle: gap BEA→OCO subiu de 2.519 s para 11.112 s após esses stops. O aumento de gap mostra que o undercut gerou vantagem de tempo na janela, pois BEA rodou volta 8 com pneus médios novos (stint 2, avg 87.49 s) enquanto OCO ainda completava sua última volta no stint 1 desgastado (avg 88.82 s).  

O undercut funcionou no quesito ganho de tempo/gap, conforme o dado direto de 2.519 s → 11.112 s.

BOR pitted lap 32 (MEDIUM→SOFT), OCO pitted lap 34 (MEDIUM→HARD). 

This 2-lap earlier stop placed BOR on fresh SOFT (stint 3, laps 33-57, avg 85.88 s) while OCO remained on MEDIUM (stint 3, laps 10-34). 

The gap BOR→OCO then widened from 2.927 s to 6.681 s. 

BOR therefore gained net track advantage via the timing; the final result (BOR P13, OCO P15) confirms the undercut succeeded.

HAM undercut OCO ao parar 1 volta antes (pit lap 7 vs. pit lap 8, ambos sob SC/VSC), saindo com MEDIUM fresco enquanto OCO ainda completava sua volta 8 em MEDIUM desgastado (Stint 1 OCO: 8 voltas, avg 88.82 s).  

O gap HAM→OCO evoluiu de 0.674 s para 5.801 s após a sequência, confirmando ganho de posição por timing.  

Funcionou: HAM manteve MEDIUM por 25 voltas (Stint 2, avg 87.68 s) com menor degradação (-0.055 s/lap) que OCO no mesmo composto equivalente (Stint 3 OCO, avg 87.82 s).

LEC undercut OCO by pitting lap 7 (MED→MED under SC/VSC) while OCO stayed out until lap 8 (MED→HARD under SC/VSC). Per the pit-timing battle data, this one-lap difference expanded the LEC→OCO gap from 3.114 s to 12.325 s.  

The undercut worked: LEC’s subsequent 25-lap MED stint averaged 87.44 s, faster than OCO’s 25-lap MED stint at 87.82 s, and LEC finished P8 ahead of OCO in P15.

TSU undercut OCO ao parar na volta 7 (MEDIUM→MEDIUM sob SC/VSC), enquanto OCO só parou na volta 9 (conforme pit-timing battle). 

O gap TSU→OCO mudou de -0.51 s para +2.29 s após esses stops. 

Isso funcionou, pois TSU terminou P10 e OCO P15, com TSU completando 25 voltas no segundo stint a 87.59 s de média contra 87.82 s de OCO no stint equivalente.

ANT used a one-stop strategy: Medium tires laps 1-19 (19 laps, avg 94.76s, deg -0.003s/lap), pit on lap 19 to Hard tires laps 20-50 (31 laps, avg 93.48s, deg -0.045s/lap). 

This covered the full 50 laps with one stop and produced the P6 finish.

NOR used a one-stop strategy: 34 laps on HARD (avg 94.06 s, deg -0.087 s/lap) then pit on lap 34 for MEDIUM over the final 16 laps (avg 92.16 s, deg +0.002 s/lap).  

The MEDIUM stint delivered a lower average lap time than the HARD stint while maintaining near-zero degradation. This performance profile across both stints produced the P4 result.

VER adotou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 em Medium (voltas 1-21, média 93.83 s, degradação +0.014 s/lap) e pit na volta 21 para Hard (voltas 22-50, média 92.81 s, degradação -0.031 s/lap). 

A troca permitiu reduzir a média de volta em 1.02 s no stint longo de 29 voltas, sustentando ritmo competitivo até o fim e resultando em P2.

BEA pitted lap 23 (MEDIUM→HARD) while HAD pitted lap 20 (SOFT→HARD), extending the first stint by 3 laps. This later timing produced the overcut, reversing the gap BEA→HAD from -1.175 s to +2.025 s. BEA finished P9 and HAD P11, confirming the overcut succeeded per the supplied gap and classification data.

ANT realizou uma estratégia de 1 pit stop: stint 1 em MEDIUM (voltas 1-25, média 97.87s, deg -0.090s/lap) e pit na volta 25 para HARD, completando stint 2 (voltas 26-62, média 96.68s, deg +0.030s/lap).

O HARD entregou média de volta mais rápida que o MEDIUM, apesar do stint 12 voltas mais longo, permitindo completar as 62 voltas e chegar em P5 conforme os dados.

NOR utilizou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 em MEDIUM (laps 1-26, 26 voltas, média 97.41s, deg -0.070s/lap) seguido de pit no lap 26 para HARD (stint 2, laps 27-62, 36 voltas, média 96.27s, deg +0.022s/lap). 

A média de volta mais baixa no HARD (96.27s) em relação ao MEDIUM, apesar da degradação positiva, sustentou o desempenho até o P3 final.

A estratégia de VER foi uma parada única: início em SOFT (voltas 1-19, 19 voltas, média 97,57 s, deg -0,081 s/vol), pit na volta 19 para HARD e continuação até a volta 62 (43 voltas, média 96,35 s, deg +0,012 s/vol).

Essa escolha moldou o resultado ao substituir o composto inicial por um stint longo em HARD com média de tempo de volta inferior (96,35 s contra 97,57 s) e degradação mínima, permitindo ritmo sustentado nas 43 voltas finais e a chegada em P2.

NOR undercut PIA by pitting on lap 26 (one lap earlier than PIA's lap 27 stop), as shown in the pit-timing data. On lap 27, NOR ran fresh HARD tires (stint 2 avg 96.27 s) while PIA completed his final MEDIUM lap (stint 1 avg 97.51 s). The gap NOR→PIA then widened from 4.458 s to 9.897 s.  

This worked: NOR finished P3 ahead of PIA in P4.

GAS executou o overcut em BEA ao realizar o pit stop na volta 55, uma volta depois do pit de BEA na volta 54 (ambos sob SC/VSC). 

Isso é fundamentado diretamente nos dados de pit lap: GAS pitted lap 55 (MEDIUM->SOFT), BEA pitted lap 54 (MEDIUM->SOFT). 

O timing produziu a variação de gap GAS->BEA de 4.73s para 54.525s. 

Funcionou, conforme o gap resultante e as posições finais informadas (GAS P8, BEA P17).

ALO adotou estratégia de 4 paradas: Soft (voltas 1-15, 15 voltas, média 83,10 s, degradação +0,274 s/volta), pit na volta 15 para Medium (voltas 16-42, 27 voltas, média 81,59 s, degradação +0,035 s/volta), pit na volta 42 para Soft (voltas 43-54, 12 voltas, média 80,36 s, degradação +0,202 s/volta) e pit na volta 54 para Soft sob SC/VSC (voltas 55-66, 12 voltas, média 79,29 s, degradação -0,473 s/volta).

A stint longa de Medium apresentou a menor degradação (+0,035 s/volta), sustentando ritmo competitivo por mais voltas que os stints de Soft. As médias de volta caíram progressivamente (83,10 s → 81,59 s → 80,36 s → 79,29 s), com a última stint de Soft mostrando tempos em melhora (degradação negativa). O pit na volta 54 sob SC/VSC permitiu troca para pneus novos sem custo significativo de tempo, viabilizando o ritmo final que resultou em P9.

HUL usou estratégia de 4 stints com 3 paradas. Começou em SOFT (laps 1-9, média 82.11 s, deg -0.047 s/lap) e parou no lap 9 para MEDIUM.  

O stint longo de MEDIUM (laps 10-45, 36 voltas, média 81.72 s, deg -0.024 s/lap) foi o de menor degradação, sustentando ritmo competitivo.  

Parou no lap 45 para SOFT (laps 46-56, média 80.45 s, deg -0.051 s/lap).  

A parada no lap 56 (SOFT→SOFT sob SC/VSC) permitiu pneus novos sem perda de tempo; o stint final (laps 57-66, média 78.34 s, deg -0.065 s/lap) registrou a menor média de volta, permitindo completar as 66 voltas em P5.

RUS usou estratégia de 4 stints: 20 voltas em SOFT (média 81.74s, deg +0.048s/lap), 21 voltas em MEDIUM (média 80.37s, deg +0.048s/lap), 14 voltas em SOFT (média 79.23s, deg +0.060s/lap) e 11 voltas em SOFT (média 78.13s, deg +0.038s/lap). Pits na volta 20 (SOFT→MEDIUM), 41 (MEDIUM→SOFT) e 55 (SOFT→SOFT sob SC/VSC). 

Os tempos médios caíram progressivamente de 81.74s para 78.13s. A degradação foi +0.048s/lap nos dois primeiros stints, subiu para +0.060 no terceiro e caiu para +0.038 no quarto. O pit da volta 55 sob SC/VSC trocou para SOFTs frescas nas voltas finais, sustentando a média mais baixa (78.13s) até o fim e resultando em P4.

ALO pitted on lap 42 (MEDIUM to SOFT) while LAW remained on MEDIUM until pitting on lap 44. This placed ALO on fresh SOFT tires for laps 43-44, during which LAW completed two additional laps on the 26-lap MEDIUM stint (avg 81.54 s).  

The gap ALO→LAW changed from -4.833 s to -1.049 s across these stops.  

The earlier stop therefore gained ALO time on LAW; the undercut succeeded on the provided timing and gap data.

BOR pitted lap 19 (SOFT→MEDIUM) while OCO remained on track until pitting lap 20 (SOFT→MEDIUM), per the listed pit laps. This one-lap-earlier stop is the undercut timing.

Post-stops, the BOR→OCO gap widened from 1.593 s to 2.768 s. BOR therefore gained net track time on the fresh MEDIUM versus OCO’s final SOFT lap, as shown directly by the gap change.

The undercut succeeded: BOR finished P12 and OCO P16.

OCO pitted lap 43 (MEDIUM->MEDIUM), one lap before TSU pitted lap 44 (SOFT->SOFT). This earlier stop timing produced the gap change OCO->TSU from -2.208 s to +0.933 s. The reversal shows the undercut succeeded on the provided pit-timing data.

TSU pitted lap 54 (SOFT->SOFT under SC/VSC) while SAI remained on track until pit lap 55 (MEDIUM->SOFT under SC/VSC). This one-lap-earlier timing is the undercut.

The provided pit-timing data directly records the outcome: gap TSU->SAI changed from -0.582 s to +3.239 s.

The undercut therefore succeeded on the timing delta alone, placing TSU ahead.

SAI executed the overcut by extending his second stint one lap longer than HAD: SAI ran medium tires on laps 18-38 (21 laps total) before pitting on lap 38, while HAD ran laps 19-37 (19 laps) and pitted on lap 37.  

This timing produced a relative gain, with the SAI-to-HAD gap improving from -4.033 s to -2.756 s.  

The overcut therefore narrowed the deficit by ~1.3 s but did not reverse final order (SAI P13, HAD P8).

A estratégia de HAD foi um two-stop: stint 1 em Soft (voltas 1-18), troca para Medium no pit 18, stint 2 em Medium (voltas 19-37) e segunda troca para Medium no pit 37, completando 34 voltas no stint 3 até a volta 71.

Os dados mostram que o Soft apresentou média mais alta (75,50 s) e degradação elevada (+0,203 s/vol), justificando a primeira troca na volta 18. Os dois stints em Medium reduziram a média para 75,01 s (degradação +0,054 s/vol) e depois 74,74 s (degradação +0,032 s/vol), permitindo ritmo mais estável nas 53 voltas totais em Medium e o resultado P8.

A estratégia de PIA foi de duas paradas: Medium por 38 voltas (laps 1-38, média 75.02 s, deg +0.025 s/lap), troca para Soft no pit lap 38, Soft por 13 voltas (39-51, média 73.62 s, deg +0.030 s/lap) e troca para Medium no pit lap 51, com Medium por 20 voltas finais (52-71, média 73.33 s, deg +0.041 s/lap).

Essa sequência produziu redução progressiva na média de voltas (de 75.02 s no primeiro stint para 73.62 s e 73.33 s nos seguintes), conforme os dados de médias por stint, permitindo ritmo mais rápido no final da prova e resultando em P5.

ALB tentou undercut ao parar no giro 35 (MEDIUM→SOFT), enquanto HAD parou apenas no giro 37 (MEDIUM→MEDIUM).  

Dados mostram que o gap ALB→HAD aumentou de 4.204 s para 5.876 s após esses pit-stops. O undercut não funcionou.

BEA realizou o undercut ao parar no lap 17 (SOFT→MEDIUM), enquanto HAD parou apenas no lap 18 (SOFT→MEDIUM). Isso colocou BEA em pneus médios frescos a partir do lap 18, ao passo que HAD ainda completava seu lap 18 no soft desgastado (stint 1 de HAD: laps 1-18).

O dado de pit-timing confirma o efeito: o gap BEA→HAD passou de -1.287 s para +3.508 s após as paradas. BEA terminou à frente (P6 vs P8 de HAD), demonstrando que o undercut funcionou.

HAD pitted lap 37 (MEDIUM) while GAS continued to lap 39 (21-lap MEDIUM stint 2, avg 75.04s). This placed HAD on fresh tires for laps 38-39 against GAS's aging set.  

The gap HAD→GAS widened from 0.697s to 2.455s across this pit-timing sequence. HAD then ran a 34-lap MEDIUM stint (avg 74.74s, deg +0.032s/lap) versus GAS's 32-lap MEDIUM stint (avg 74.72s, deg +0.044s/lap).  

The undercut succeeded per the recorded gap increase and final positions (HAD P8, GAS P10).

SAI undercut LAW ao parar na volta 17 (pit lap 17, SOFT→MEDIUM), dois giros antes de LAW (pit lap 19, SOFT→MEDIUM).  

Isso produziu a variação de gap SAI→LAW de -2.302 s para -0.794 s.  

O undercut funcionou: o gap reduziu 1,508 s com o timing de parada.

HUL overcut BEA by extending stint 1 to lap 33 on medium (33 laps total, avg 100.71 s) while BEA pitted on lap 30 after only 30 laps on medium.  

During laps 31–33, HUL remained on medium and closed the gap to BEA from –1.685 s to –0.387 s.  

HUL then switched to soft on lap 33 and ran 23 laps (avg 99.39 s), finishing P8 ahead of BEA’s P9. The later stop therefore gained net track position.

HAM adotou estratégia de uma parada: stint 1 de 31 voltas em Medium (média 100.30s, degradação +0.001s/lap), parada na volta 31 para Soft e stint 2 de 25 voltas (média 99.04s, degradação +0.062s/lap). 

A baixa degradação no Medium permitiu ritmo consistente nas primeiras 31 voltas; a troca para Soft gerou média 1.26s mais rápida no stint final, apesar da degradação mais alta, resultando em P4 ao final das 56 voltas.

PIA usou estratégia de dois stints: Stint 1 em MEDIUM (laps 1-30, 30 voltas, média 100.44s, deg -0.000s/lap) seguido de pit no lap 30 para SOFT (Stint 2: laps 31-56, 26 voltas, média 98.91s, deg -0.023s/lap). O composto MEDIUM manteve ritmo estável sem degradação por 30 voltas; a troca para SOFT reduziu a média em 1.53s por volta na parte final, apesar da leve degradação, resultando em P5.

COL undercut BOR ao parar na volta 32 (MEDIUM→SOFT), enquanto BOR ainda cumpria o stint de MEDIUM até parar na volta 36.  

Dados: COL completou 32 voltas no MEDIUM (média 101.72 s) e trocou para SOFT na volta 32; BOR rodou as voltas 15-36 no MEDIUM (média 100.84 s) antes de trocar para SOFT na volta 36.  

Com pneus SOFT novos a partir da volta 33, COL registrou média de 100.04 s no stint 2, contra a média de 100.84 s de BOR ainda no MEDIUM envelhecido.  

O gap COL→BOR passou de 0.835 s para 6.143 s após esses pit stops.  

O undercut funcionou: o gap aumentou 5.308 s e COL terminou à frente (P17 vs P18).

PIA pitted on lap 30 (MEDIUM->SOFT) while RUS remained on MEDIUM until lap 33, per the stint data.  

This earlier stop produced the observed pit-timing gap change from 1.324 s to 4.016 s.  

The undercut succeeded: PIA finished P5 and RUS finished P6.

ALB realizó un overcut exitoso sobre ANT basándose en los datos de ritmo y deg.  
- **Pit timing**: ALB pitted en la vuelta 33, ANT en la 32. ALB hizo una vuelta extra en neumáticos duros antes de su segunda parada.  
- **Cambio de gap**: El gap ALB→ANT pasó de -2.902s (antes de paradas) a -2.312s (después). Como ALB estaba detrás (gap negativo), la reducción en valor absoluto indica que ALB perdió menos tiempo del esperado.  
- **Ritmo de la vuelta extra**: ALB en la vuelta 33 (última en HARD, avg 90.16s) mantuvo un ritmo competitivo frente al HARD de ANT en la vuelta 32 (avg ~90.81s). Esto minimizó la pérdida frente a ANT, quien en la vuelta 33 salió en MEDIUM pero probablemente aún en fase de calentamiento.  
- **Resultado**: El overcut funcionó porque ALB emergió más cerca de ANT tras las paradas, mejorando su posición relativa.

La estrategia de HUL fue una **parada a dos, comenzando en SOFT para un stint corto inicial, luego HARD por un stint largo central, y finalizando en MEDIUM.**  

- **Stint 1 (SOFT, laps 1-7):**  
  Se eligió el SOFT para obtener un buen arranque y posición inicial, pero mostró degradación significativa (-0.285s/vuelta). Esto motivó el pit temprano en la vuelta 7, antes de que el ritmo cayera demasiado.

- **Stint 2 (HARD, laps 8-42):**  
  El HARD fue muy estable (+0.029s/vuelta de degradación), permitiendo un stint largo de 35 vueltas para llegar a la ventana de una segunda parada. El promedio (90.01s) fue más rápido que el stint inicial en SOFT, confirmando que fue el compuesto correcto para la fase central.

- **Stint 3 (MEDIUM, laps 43-58):**  
  En las últimas 16 vueltas, el MEDIUM ofreció buen ritmo (avg 88.09s, el más rápido de sus stint) y degradación manejable (+0.101s/vuelta). Esto le permitió atacar en la fase final y consolidar el P9.

**Influencia en el resultado:**  
La secuencia SOFT-HARD-MEDIUM optimizó el uso de compuestos para cubrir 58 vueltas con dos paradas, gestionando bien la degradación. El stint largo en HARD le dio flexibilidad para parar en la vuelta 42 sin riesgo de desgaste excesivo al final. Terminar en MEDIUM le permitió mantener un ritmo competitivo hasta la bandera, asegurando puntos en la P9.

PIA usó una estrategia de una parada: comenzó con HARD y cambió a MEDIUM en la vuelta 41.  
La estrategia fue eficaz porque:

1. **Largo primer stint en HARD**: Las gomas HARD tuvieron un desgaste muy bajo (-0.018s/vuelta), permitiendo 41 vueltas a un ritmo estable (avg 89.41s) sin perder mucho tiempo por degradación. Esto extendió el stint y permitió un tramo final más corto.
2. **Ritmo rápido en MEDIUM al final**: Con el MEDIUM en las últimas 17 vueltas, PIA fue más rápido (avg 87.44s) que con el HARD, aprovechando el compuesto más rápido con bajo combustible. Aunque la degradación fue positiva (+0.081s/vuelta), el coche era suficientemente rápido para mantener o mejorar la posición.
3. **Minimización del tiempo total**: Al hacer una sola parada y mantener un ritmo consistente en ambos compuestos, PIA logró terminar en P2. La elección del MEDIUM al final permitió atacar o defender en la fase decisiva sin comprometer la durabilidad.

Los datos muestran que la estrategia optimizó el equilibrio entre durabilidad inicial y velocidad final, contribuyendo al resultado de P2.

BEA ejecutó un undercut exitoso al adelantar su parada 4 vueltas antes que SAI.

**Explicación y resultado:**
1. **Timing:** BEA entró a boxes en la vuelta 14, mientras SAI permaneció en pista hasta la vuelta 18. BEA cambió de MEDIUM a HARD, aprovechando que sus neumáticos MEDIUM ya mostraban degradación (+0.032s/vuelta).
2. **Funcionamiento:** La brecha inicial de BEA sobre SAI era de +0.811s antes de la parada. Al parar primero, BEA circuló con HARD nuevos y rápidos (promedio 89.64s) mientras SAI seguía con MEDIUM degradados (avg 90.80s, deg +0.042s/vuelta). Esto permitió que BEA superara a SAI "virtualmente".
3. **Resultado:** Después de que SAI completó su parada (vuelta 18), la nueva brecha aumentó a +7.067s a favor de BEA. Por lo tanto, el undercut funcionó claramente: BEA pasó de estar apenas delante a crear una ventaja significativa, lo que contribuyó a su final P12 vs. P13 de SAI.

HAD realizó un undercut sobre SAI al hacer su primera parada en la vuelta 14, cuatro vueltas antes que SAI (vuelta 18). Los datos muestran que, al momento de la parada de HAD, el gap era de 4.686s. Después de que ambos completaron sus paradas, el gap aumentó a 9.108s, lo que demuestra que el undercut fue exitoso, ya que HAD salió de boxes con una ventaja mayor que la que tenía antes de parar.

OCO ejecutó un undercut exitoso sobre LAW al detenerse dos vueltas antes (vuelta 19 vs 21). Esto funcionó porque:

1. **Timing de la parada**: OCO pit en vuelta 19, LAW en 21.
2. **Rendimiento en pista**: En las vueltas 20 y 21 (mientras LAW seguía con neumáticos MEDIUM ya degradados, con un degaste de +0.078s/vuelta), OCO circuló con HARD nuevos y rápidos (promedio 89.41s).
